message:
fix(stdlib): close getenv vs setenv UAF on HOST_ENVIRON realloc

REVIEW round 3: native_getenv walked HOST_ENVIRON without acquiring
ENVIRON_LOCK while native_setenv / native_putenv path realloc that
same array via host_passthrough_realloc. A reader iterating its
local cursor through the old array memory after a concurrent setenv
moved the array dereferenced freed memory — UAF.

The static `static mut HOST_ENVIRON` access without synchronization
is also a Rust data race even ignoring the realloc move semantics.

Fix: native_getenv now takes ENVIRON_LOCK around the array walk so
the layout is stable for the duration of the lookup. The lock is
released before returning the entry-value pointer; callers that
retain the pointer across a subsequent setenv accept POSIX's
documented "value may be overwritten" semantics, which the existing
implementation already honors (replace and remove paths orphan the
old entry's bytes rather than freeing them).

Promoted ENVIRON_LOCK from std::sync::Mutex to
parking_lot::ReentrantMutex because the setenv path calls
host_passthrough_malloc / host_passthrough_realloc; on first invocation
glibc's malloc init can probe MALLOC_PERTURB_/MALLOC_CHECK_ via getenv
and recursively re-enter our wrapper. A non-reentrant Mutex would
self-deadlock that early-startup window.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass (no test regression; 202/202 stdlib
tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
